73884.subject: 📈 Hyperliquid giga-sends to new highs
73884.body:
Today’s newsletter is brought to you by **[Choose Rich Live](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)**[!](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)

Our very own Nick O’Neill is has launched a new stream covering crypto, finance, AI, tech, and the stories shaping the internet economy.

If you already follow Nick, you know what to expect: sharp takes, market chaos, founder brain, and the occasional banger you’ll see clipped on the timeline five minutes later.

Episodes will be streaming live on YouTube, so make sure you’re subscribed to catch the next one as it happens.

**[Subscribe to Choose Rich Live!](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)**

———————————————————————————

## Top Stories

**BTC** $72,354 (-2.0%) | **ETH** $1,979 (-2.0%) | **SOL** $81 (-2.2%)

* Binance launches “[“bStocks”](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2061382232686891264?s=20) tokenized equities program

* Hyperliquid hits new [ATH over $73](https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/hyperliquid/) as the [HYPE/BTC ratio](https://x.com/Tyler_Did_It/status/2061408416577282413?s=20) hits 0.001 for the first time ever

* US conducts [“self-defense strikes”](https://x.com/KobeissiLetter/status/2061293916964491332?s=20) against Iranian targets — [Seizes over $1B](https://x.com/zoomerfied/status/2060439817716945164?s=20) of Iranian crypto

* OpenAI begins hiring [robotics engineers](https://x.com/sama/status/2061117302528188712?s=20)

* [IBM surges over 12%](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/IBM/) in premarket trading

* Nvidia enters consumer PC chip market with [RTX Spark](https://www.theverge.com/tech/940589/nvidia-rtx-spark-n1-n1x-laptop-desktop-pc-cpu-gpu-ai-release-date) which powers Microsoft’s new [Surface laptop](https://www.theverge.com/tech/940584/microsoft-surface-laptop-ultra-nvidia-rtx-spark-pictures)

* Nvidia CEO says Microsoft and Nvidia will [reinvent the PC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/31/nvidias-new-chip-to-power-fresh-line-of-windows-laptops-by-dell-hp.html)

* Spot BTC ETFs see [$1.4B](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2061296895067402348?s=20) in net weekly outflows

* Meta outlines plans for [wearable pendant](https://x.com/tradfi/status/2060507182744182890?s=20) coming 2h 2026

* Trump calls for Fort Knox [gold audit](https://x.com/KobeissiLetter/status/2061049048363311443?s=20)

* Coinbase launches [global derivatives markets](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2060400985131143274?s=20) for US institutions

* Kalshi launches the [American Power Index](https://x.com/DeItaone/status/2060074471617437891?s=20) which tracks political party influence in the US

* Claude’s Opus 4.8 underperforms GPT 5.5 but uses [much less code](https://x.com/teortaxesTex/status/2061406883882156434)

* [90% of VC funding](https://x.com/KobeissiLetter/status/2061282690712637788?s=20) is going towards AI

* Solana prints its [8th straight](https://x.com/DegenerateNews/status/2061395320299286532?s=20) monthly red candle

Fear and Greed is at
33
(Fear),
and BTC is at
$73K
to open June. May closed in the red, with BTC down roughly 3.65%
for the month as geopolitical deal optimism, inflation fears, and
record ETF outflows combined to weigh on prices

June opens with a genuine policy shift. The CFTC just authorised
perpetual futures for US traders for the first time. Social
sentiment hit its highest reading of 2026 this week, even as
prices fell. Strategy is signalling another buy. The market is in
extreme fear, but the regulatory calendar just moved
significantly in crypto's favour.

Let's dive in.

Latest News
-----------

Strategy Is Signalling Another Bitcoin Buy

Michael Saylor posted his standard pre-buy chart on May 31
captioned "Working Better," a signal that has preceded every
Strategy Bitcoin purchase for six years. May closed with BTC down
roughly 3.65%.

Any new purchase would fall below Strategy's average acquisition
cost of $75,701 per coin.

What has Strategy's average Bitcoin acquisition cost been across
all purchases?

Read More  
( https://link.coinmarketcap.com/f/a/Yrb3MWEJ01BZgPuEs0kzKw~~/AAQRxRA~/MIlBbauFsMFTX5oakhPWZJNI80VE1XtrikUBU0hzEIoy5xyKh1Jd5V04g_2S3UvUROjWQQFyz9JbK6ePbHYQXhvXNSPhPtRNYCt--0hKIXSTMXzaEPjonClX75dnP3FeYPjJ5pcfD7k9ovT7XO3Hcw2imqd5osiQzFpUyUFmRpc~ )
   Bitcoin Social Sentiment Hit a Year High as Prices Fell

Bullish Bitcoin comments outnumbered bearish ones by 2

73878.subject: Should you use a sleep tracker?
73878.body:
The Economist’s week ahead

Автор: The Economist


Автор: The Economist

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/should-you-use-sleep-tracker-the-economist-ntwre?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BHXMxTRQzSoi65LNLKb5u%2Fg%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=0b3dNYTpKVMsg1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mpv39xv7~q8-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mpv39xv7-q8
  
Enjoying this newsletter? Get it in your inbox every weekend, as well as our daily round-up of The Economist’s best journalism, by signing up for free here. Trackers—smartwatches, smart rings, phone apps—can tell you how much shut-eye you actually got, and perhaps log how long you spent in different sleep stages. But more data has downsides: fretting about rest is one of the most common causes of a sleepless night. Last week ExxonMobil’s shareholders voted to cut ties with New Jersey and reincorporate in Texas. The oil major is only the latest company to shift its headquarters to the Lone Star State. From Dallas’s “Y’all Street” to Austin’s “Silicon Hills”, Texas is steadily establishing itself as America Inc’s new centre of gravity.  What to expect in the week ahead: ▸ Ethiopia heads to the polls on Monday. Abiy Ahmed, the prime minister, is certain to be re-elected. Mr Abiy once promised a historic transition to democracy. Instead, through guile and brute force, he has personalised power to a degree unseen since the country’s last emperor. ▸The euro area’s inflation data for May will be published on Tuesday. Its annual inflation rate has been creeping up this year, hitting 3% in April, as a consequence of higher energy prices induced by the war in Iran. ▸ More American states hold primary elections on Tuesday, including California. Voters will choose the top two candidates for the state’s next governor. Gavin Newsom, the incumbent, cannot run again because of term limits. More than a dozen Democrats are running, among them Tom Steyer, a billionaire. Annie Crabill, Senior digital editor  Editor’s picks Should you use a sleep tracker?
